descriptions: Chichesters were allies of Tammany Hall per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Dead Rabbits , Tammany Hall

descriptions: Tammany Hall supported the Five Points Gang politically.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tammany Hall

descriptions: The Forty Thieves established relations with Tammany Hall, a corrupt bureaucracy.
edges.evidence_quote: The Forty Thieves saw this as an economic opportunity, as they established relations with Tammany Hall.

'Ndrina Abbruzzese.description: The 'Ndrina Abbruzzese is a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cassano allo Ionio and Corigliano Calabro, Italy. It emerged in the late 1990s, known for armored car robberies and its members being of Romani ethnicity. The group was involved in a war with other 'ndrine from 1999 to 2001 and has been implicated in drug trafficking and murder, including the 2014 killing of three-year-old Cocò Campolongo.

descriptions: The Abbruzzese is a clan (ndrina) belonging to the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Abbruzzese è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: The Abbruzzese attempted to involve the families of Cirò in their conflict with Cosenza.
edges.evidence_quote: Di conseguenza il clan degli zingari tentò di coinvolgere le famiglie di Cirò e Corigliano Calabro e far ricadere la colpa su Cosenza.

descriptions: The Abbruzzese attempted to involve the families of Corigliano Calabro in their conflict with Cosenza.
edges.evidence_quote: Di conseguenza il clan degli zingari tentò di coinvolgere le famiglie di Cirò e Corigliano Calabro e far ricadere la colpa su Cosenza.

descriptions: The Abbruzzese (clan degli zingari) fought a war with the 'ndrine of Cosenza from 1999 to 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1999 e fino al 2001 a causa delle mire espansionistiche del clan degli zingari scoppia una guerra con le 'ndrine di Cosenza.

descriptions: The Abbruzzese and Forastefano were both targeted in the same police operation, indicating a connection.
edges.evidence_quote: 16 febbraio 2021: operazione Kossa contro i Forastefano e gli Abbruzzese.

descriptions: The two clans clashed in a fight that led to a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 10 ottobre 1976 viene ucciso a Milano, a seguito di una rissa, Giuseppe Di Silvio ... L'omicidio matura in uno scontro tra il "clan dei calabresi" e il "clan degli zingari".

descriptions: Nicola Acri, boss of 'Ndrina Acri, is linked to the Abbruzzese clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Nicola Acri ... legato agli Abbruzzese di Cassano allo Ionio

descriptions: Forastefano and Abbruzzese were originally one single 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: In origine era un'unica 'ndrina insieme agli Abbruzzese

descriptions: Hells Angels absorbed the remnants of the Crew to form a new chapter of Independent Soldiers.
edges.evidence_quote: After the police arrested several members of the Crew including Payne, the Hells Angels took what was left of the Crew to merge with criminals from Kelowna to found the new Prince George chapter of the Independent Soldiers.

descriptions: Independent Soldiers replaced the Crew as Hells Angels puppet gang in Prince George.
edges.evidence_quote: When a puppet gang for the Hells Angels in Prince George known as 'the Crew' were broken up by police arrests, the Hells Angels had the Independent Soldiers establish a chapter in Prince George as the replacement puppet gang for 'the Crew'.

descriptions: Renegades founded the Crew as a puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels founded a puppet gang in Prince George known as the Renegades who in turn founded another puppet gang known as the Crew.

Aprea-Cuccaro clan.description: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is a Camorra criminal organization operating in Barra, Naples, founded in the early 1990s by Giovanni Aprea and Angelo Cuccaro. It is involved in murder, extortion, drug trafficking, racketeering, and money laundering. The clan has allied with the Secondigliano Alliance and the De Luca Bossa clan, and has been engaged in feuds with the Mazzarella and Sarno clans. It remains active as of the 2000s.

descriptions: The Andolfi faction is linked to the Cuccaros within the Aprea-Cuccaro clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Ciro Imperatrice, known as Brutolino, was arrested in an apartment in Barra, the police surprised him hidden inside a closet of the bedroom. Imperatrice is allegedly the regent of the Andolfi faction, particularly linked to the Cuccaros.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan fought a violent feud with the Celeste-Guarino clan in 2005-2006.
edges.evidence_quote: Aprea-Cuccaro clan and Celeste-Guarino clan : A violent feud fought in the area of Barra, between the Aprea-Cuccaro clan and what according to investigators, was the splitting faction of the Celeste-Guarino in the years 2005 and 2006.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan has the Lago clan as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mazzarella clan , Sarno clan (defunct) , Lago clan (defunct) , Formicola clan

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan opposed the Sarno clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The goal was to resist the pressure exerted by the Mazzarella clan and the Sarno clan.

descriptions: Giovanni Aprea worked for the Sarno clan early in his career.
edges.evidence_quote: Giovanni Aprea, known as ' e curtiello , a leading figure of the clan, began the criminal career working for Ciro Sarno , then a powerful boss of the Sarno clan from Ponticelli.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan supports the D'Atri faction.
edges.evidence_quote: And through contacts with the local criminals, would have taken control of illicit affairs in the area, supporting the D'Atri faction, a small criminal group already inserted in the underworld of Somma Vesuviana.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan supported the De Luca Bossa clan against the Sarno clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The clan, in fact, is the first to offer support to the De Luca Bossa clan against the Sarnos, in the times of the violent feud between the two organizations.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan formed a criminal alliance with the De Luca Bossa clan.
edges.evidence_quote: This new wave of extortions made by the clan are considered part of the new criminal alliance between the Aprea clan, the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the De Luca Bossa clan to undermine the Mazzarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the latest DIA reports, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the Rinaldi clan from San Giovanni a Teduccio , and with the De Luca Bossa clan from Ponticelli , with the intention of undermining the Mazzarella clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan formed a new criminal alliance with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the new criminal alliance formed with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan was in a bloody feud with the Formicola clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Aprea-Cuccaro clan and Formicola clan : According to investigators, one of the bloodiest feuds in the eastern area of the city of Naples.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan opposed the Mazzarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The goal was to resist the pressure exerted by the Mazzarella clan and the Sarno clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan intends to undermine the Mazzarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the latest DIA reports, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the Rinaldi clan from San Giovanni a Teduccio , and with the De Luca Bossa clan from Ponticelli , with the intention of undermining the Mazzarella clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan formed a criminal alliance with the Rinaldi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: This new wave of extortions made by the clan are considered part of the new criminal alliance between the Aprea clan, the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the Rinaldi clan to undermine the Mazzarella clan.
edges.evidence_quote: According to the latest DIA reports, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan is allied with the Rinaldi clan from San Giovanni a Teduccio , and with the De Luca Bossa clan from Ponticelli , with the intention of undermining the Mazzarella clan.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan formed a new criminal alliance with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the new criminal alliance formed with the De Luca Bossa clan and the Rinaldi clan

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan made an agreement with the Secondigliano Alliance since the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: According to investigators, since the 1990s, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan has entered into an agreement with the Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: Infobox lists Aprea-Cuccaro clan as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Marfella clan, Rinaldi clan, Secondigliano Alliance, Aprea-Cuccaro clan

descriptions: De Luca Bossa was protected by ties with Aprea-Cuccaro.
edges.evidence_quote: i De Luca Bossa, insieme ai loro alleati, avrebbero trasferito la loro roccaforte nella vicina Barra ‘protetti’ dai legami con gli Aprea-Cuccaro

descriptions: Mazzarella clan is rival of Aprea-Cuccaro clan according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Secondigliano Alliance Contini clan , Licciardi clan , Mallardo clan , Rinaldi clan , De Luca Bossa clan , Vollaro clan , Aprea-Cuccaro clan

descriptions: Rinaldi clan formed an alliance with Aprea-Cuccaro clan since 2019.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2019 the clan has formed an alliance with the Ponticelli 's De Luca Bossa clan , and Barra 's Aprea-Cuccaro clan , called by the media Rinaldi-De Luca Bossa-Aprea

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan is a founding clan and ally of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Founding clans : Contini clan , Licciardi clan , Mallardo clan , Di Lauro clan , Rinaldi clan , De Luca Bossa clan , Aprea-Cuccaro clan , Commisso 'ndrina

descriptions: Apachenblut is one of the clubs that joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu" ("ever faithful"), "Libelle" ("Dragonfly"), "Deutsche Eiche" (German oak), or "Apachenblut" ("blood of the Apache").

descriptions: Berliner is one of the clubs that joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu" ("ever faithful"), "Libelle" ("Dragonfly"), "Deutsche Eiche" (German oak), or "Apachenblut" ("blood of the Apache").

descriptions: Deutsche Eiche is one of the clubs that joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu" ("ever faithful"), "Libelle" ("Dragonfly"), "Deutsche Eiche" (German oak), or "Apachenblut" ("blood of the Apache").

descriptions: Immertreu is one of the clubs that joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu" ("ever faithful"), "Libelle" ("Dragonfly"), "Deutsche Eiche" (German oak), or "Apachenblut" ("blood of the Apache").

descriptions: Libelle is one of the clubs that joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The criminal clubs of the 1920s had poetic names such as "Berliner", "Immertreu" ("ever faithful"), "Libelle" ("Dragonfly"), "Deutsche Eiche" (German oak), or "Apachenblut" ("blood of the Apache").

descriptions: Ringvereine is the union that emerged when clubs joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The term emerged for a union with criminal orientation when several clubs by former prisoners joined the holding organization "Ring Berlin".

descriptions: Cash Money Brothers is a splinter faction that left the Indian Posse.
edges.evidence_quote: Clarence 'Shrimpy' Williams to leave the gang to found a new gang called the Cash Money Brothers.

Contras.description: The Contras were anti-communist rebel groups that waged a guerrilla war against the Sandinista government in Nicaragua from 1979 to 1990. They were formed by dissidents and former National Guard members, and received military and financial aid from the United States and other countries. The Contras were known for human rights violations and terrorism against civilians. The insurgency ended with a negotiated demobilization in 1990.

descriptions: ARDE Frente Sur is a constituent group of the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: F.D.N., A.R.D.E. Frente Sur, YATAMA, Misura

descriptions: A.R.D.E. Frente Sur is a constituent group of the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: F.D.N., A.R.D.E. Frente Sur, YATAMA, Misura

descriptions: Misura is a constituent group of the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: F.D.N., A.R.D.E. Frente Sur, YATAMA, Misura

descriptions: The UNO was a political coalition that opposed the Sandinistas, but the text does not assert a direct relationship between the Contras and UNO beyond shared opposition.
edges.evidence_quote: A united opposition of 14 political parties organized into the National Opposition Union (Unión Nacional Oppositora, UNO) with the support of the United States National Endowment for Democracy.

descriptions: The Contras fought a guerrilla war against the FSLN government.
edges.evidence_quote: The Contras ... were the anti-communist rebels who waged a guerrilla war against the Marxist Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN)

descriptions: The Contras fought a guerrilla war against the Sandinista government.
edges.evidence_quote: The Contras (from Spanish: contrarrevolucionarios, lit. 'counter-revolutionaries') were the anti-communist rebels who waged a guerrilla war against the Marxist Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN)

descriptions: The Contras waged a guerrilla war against the Sandinista government.
edges.evidence_quote: The Contras (from Spanish: contrarrevolucionarios, lit. 'counter-revolutionaries') were the anti-communist rebels who waged a guerrilla war against the Marxist Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN) and the Junta of National Reconstruction, which came to power after the Nicaraguan Revolution in 1979.

descriptions: The Enterprise was created to secretly fund and supply the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: With the third-party funds, North created an organization called The Enterprise, which served as the secret arm of the NSC staff and had its own airplanes, pilots, airfield, ship, operatives, and secret Swiss bank accounts.

descriptions: The FDN was part of the reorganization into UNO.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 1985 most of the groups reorganized as the United Nicaraguan Opposition (UNO)

descriptions: YATAMA is a constituent group of the Contras.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: F.D.N., A.R.D.E. Frente Sur, YATAMA, Misura

descriptions: The 15 September Legion merged into the FDN in 1981.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1981, the CIA and Argentina's intelligence agency ... persuaded 15 September Legion, the UDN, and several former smaller groups to merge as the Nicaraguan Democratic Force

descriptions: Infobox lists Contras as allies of La Corporación.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bolivian military junta, Medellín Cartel, Mexican drug cartels, Contras

descriptions: Misura aligned closely with the FDN.
edges.evidence_quote: The Misurasata movement split in 1983, with the breakaway Misura group ... which aligned more closely with the FDN

descriptions: The UDN merged into the FDN in 1981.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 1981, the CIA and Argentina's intelligence agency ... persuaded 15 September Legion, the UDN, and several former smaller groups to merge as the Nicaraguan Democratic Force

descriptions: The RN succeeded UNO as the umbrella group for Contra factions.
edges.evidence_quote: After UNO's dissolution early in 1987, the Nicaraguan Resistance (RN) was organized along similar lines in May.

descriptions: CJNG allied with local groups to form Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala.
edges.evidence_quote: Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala

descriptions: Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala was formed to oppose Los Huistas' hegemony.
edges.evidence_quote: En 2024-2025, surgieron tensiones cuando Los Huistas comenzaron a cobrar "impuestos" por el paso de drogas a otros grupos locales, como la banda de "Tío Balde". Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala, buscando hacer frente a la hegemonía de Los Huistas.

descriptions: Tío Balde band joined with CJNG to form Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala.
edges.evidence_quote: Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala

descriptions: ACC fought a bloody war with Bloque Centauros for control of Casanare.
edges.evidence_quote: Entre 2003 y 2004 se desató de manera muy cruenta el conflicto entre las ACC con el Bloque Centauros de Arroyave por el control del departamento de Casanare.

descriptions: The Centauros clashed with the ACC.
edges.evidence_quote: the Centauros soon began clashing with a rival paramilitary group, the Peasant Self-Defense Forces of Casanare (ACC).

descriptions: Bloque Centauros was an ally of AUC, acting to defeat ACC.
edges.evidence_quote: La idea de las AUC (Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia) era derrotar a ‘Martín Llanos’ y controlar además del territorio, las rutas del narcotráfico.

descriptions: Bloque Vencedores de Arauca supported Miguel Arroyave's Bloque Centauros against ACC.
edges.evidence_quote: Algunas versiones apuntan a asegurar que el Bloque Vencedores de Arauca (otro aliado de las AUC) llegó por el norte para apoyar a las tropas de Miguel Arroyave

descriptions: The AGC's origins trace back to the Bloque Centauros, as its founder Don Mario worked for them.
edges.evidence_quote: the origins of the group can be traced elsewhere, in Colombia's Eastern Plains, where Daniel Rendón Herrera, better known as ‘Don Mario,’ once handled finances for the paramilitary group Bloque Centauros.

descriptions: Sergi used the Moscardi family's gas station for drug distribution.
edges.evidence_quote: il distributore Esso in via Milano a Corsico, gestito dalla famiglia Moscardi

descriptions: Sons of Samoa has a rivalry with P.V.C. (Park Village Crip).

descriptions: The satellite clans La Torre and Esposito killed Alberto Beneduce, a Casalesi member, indicating conflict.
time_periods: fine degli anni ottanta e inizio degli anni novanta
edges.evidence_quote: l'omicidio di Alberto Beneduce, operato dai clan satelliti La Torre ed Esposito

descriptions: Esposito and Ascione fought a war in 1990, with Esposito losing.
edges.evidence_quote: prima faida di Ercolano : guerra tra gli Esposito e gli Ascione, combattuta quasi interamente nel 1990

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tijuana Cartel, Juárez Cartel, 'Ndrangheta, Los Mazatlecos, Barrio Azteca, MS-13, Texas Syndicate, Triad, The Office of Envigado, La Línea, Hezbollah, Sicilian Mafia, Los Zetas Group Bravo

descriptions: Hezbollah created the Lebanese Brigades to Fight the Israeli Occupation in 1997.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, Hezbollah formed the multi-confessional Lebanese Brigades to Fight the Israeli Occupation

descriptions: Frappaolo is a splinter group from Pecoraro-Renna.
edges.evidence_quote: All'interno del clan si sarebbero verificate delle scissioni, a séguito delle quali sarebbero sorti due gruppi autonomi facenti capo alle famiglie Trimarco, Frappaolo e Giffoni.

descriptions: Franco and Polifroni were allied in the Faida di Ciminà.
edges.evidence_quote: scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Ciminà in cui vengono coinvolte le famiglie Barillaro-Romano-Zucco da una parte, e i Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo dall'altra

descriptions: Varacalli and Polifroni were allied in the Faida di Ciminà.
edges.evidence_quote: scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Ciminà in cui vengono coinvolte le famiglie Barillaro-Romano-Zucco da una parte, e i Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo dall'altra

edges.evidence_quote: scoppia la cosiddetta faida di Ciminà in cui vengono coinvolte le famiglie Barillaro-Romano-Zucco da una parte, e i Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo dall'altra

'Ndrina Mangeruca.description: The 'Ndrina Mangeruca is a 'ndrina (family) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Cornaredo near Milan, Italy, with origins in Cirò Marina. Active since the 1970s, they operate in construction, furniture, and public establishments such as bars and restaurants. Members have been involved in extortion and aggravated robbery, with recent arrests and trials.

descriptions: The Mangeruca are a 'ndrina (sub-unit) of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mangeruca sono una 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta

descriptions: The Iovine faction is a faction within the Casalesi.
edges.evidence_quote: Mentre per la fazione Iovine, secondo la DDA risulta essere quasi totalmente assente dopo il pentimento del boss Antonio Iovine

Newton Gang.description: The Newton Gang was an outlaw group active from around 1919 to 1924, primarily engaged in train and bank robberies across the United States and Canada. The gang consisted of four Newton brothers and Brent Glasscock, and they claimed to have robbed 75 banks and six trains without killing anyone. Their most famous crime was the 1924 Rondout, Illinois train robbery, which netted $3 million. The gang's activities declined after the 1920s, with members later involved in other crimes.

descriptions: Newton Gang members provided hideout for Barrow Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: After the April 6, 1934, murder of Constable Cal Campbell by Clyde Barrow and Henry Methvin in Commerce, Oklahoma, Joe and Willis Newton allowed the Barrow Gang to hide out in a house they owned in Tulsa.

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are the precursors to SNS, meaning SNS succeeded them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to most, if not all, Detroit gangs that followed in their wake. Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9"

Clan Parisi.description: The Parisi-Palermiti clan is a criminal organization based in Bari, Italy, with strongholds in the Japigia neighborhood. Founded in the 1980s by Savino Parisi and Eugenio Palermiti, it controls drug trafficking, extortion, and other illicit activities. The clan has infiltrated businesses and local politics, and has been involved in violent conflicts with rival groups. Despite the imprisonment of its founder, the organization remains active under the leadership of Eugenio Palermiti.

descriptions: Bellocco is allied with the Parisi-Palermiti clan according to the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Parisi-Palermiti clan

descriptions: Bellocco and Parisi were both involved in the same illegal betting operation, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: 14 novembre 2018 si conclude l'operazione Galassia ... in cui il presunto membro dei Pesce-Bellocco Antonio Zungri, alcuni membri dei Tegano e forse dei Piromalli insieme a camorristi e a criminali pugliesi (clan Capriati e Parisi) si erano inseriti illegalmente nella rete commerciale delle società di scommesse online

descriptions: Pesce-Bellocco cooperated with clan Parisi in illegal online betting.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 14 novembre 2018 si conclude l'operazione "Galassia" ... in cui il presunto membro dei Pesce- Bellocco Antonio Zungri, alcuni membri dei Tegano e forse dei Piromalli insieme a camorristi e a criminali pugliesi (clan Capriati e Parisi) si erano inseriti illegalmente nella rete commerciale delle società di scommesse online

descriptions: Piromalli cooperated with Capriati and Parisi in illegal betting.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 14 novembre 2018 si conclude l'operazione Galassia ... in cui il presunto membro dei Pesce-Bellocco Antonio Zungri, alcuni membri dei Tegano e forse dei Piromalli insieme a camorristi e a criminali pugliesi (clan Capriati e Parisi) si erano inseriti illegalmente nella rete commerciale delle società di scommesse online

descriptions: Tegano and Parisi are mentioned together in illegal gambling operations.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 14 novembre 2018 si conclude l'operazione Galassia della Guardia di Finanza , della DIA , della polizia e dei carabinieri in cui il presunto membro dei Pesce - Bellocco Antonio Zungri, alcuni membri dei Tegano e forse dei Piromalli insieme a camorristi , ai Santapaola - Ercolano di Catania e a criminali pugliesi ( clan Capriati e Parisi ) si erano inseriti illegalmente nella rete commerciale delle società di scommesse online "Planetwin365", "Betaland", "Enjoybet" e "Planetwin".

descriptions: Albanian mafia and Parisi-Palermiti clan had a 'communion of interests' in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The investigation focused on large quantities of heroin and cocaine moved since 2016 between the Balkans , Northern Europe , South America , and the region of Apulia . Investigators reconstructed what was described as a “communion of interests” between the Albanian mafia, responsible at a transnational level for the commercialization and transfer of narcotics, and the Parisi-Palermiti clan.

descriptions: Albanian mafia connected with Parisi-Palermiti clan in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2026, the Italian police launched the “Operazione Ura”, which uncovered connections between the Albanian mafia and the Parisi-Palermiti clan active in Bari.

descriptions: The Capriati clan is allied with the Parisi-Palermiti clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Parisi-Palermiti clan , Mercante-Diomede clan , Di Cosola clan , Bellocco 'ndrina

descriptions: The two clans jointly set fire to the Teatro Petruzzelli.
edges.evidence_quote: la notte del 27 ottobre 1991 i clan Parisi e Capriati incendiarono il celebre Teatro Petruzzelli di Bari

descriptions: Clan Parisi is described as a historical ally of Clan Capriati, providing support.
edges.evidence_quote: ha avuto anche il sostegno di Tommaso Parisi, figlio di Savino Parisi, boss del clan Parisi, storico alleato dei Capriati.

descriptions: Parisi had close ties with the 'Ndrangheta for drug supply.
edges.evidence_quote: aveva stretti legami con la 'Ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: The Bellocco 'ndrina is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Capriati clan , Mercante-Diomede clan , Di Cosola clan , Bellocco 'ndrina , Albanian mafia

descriptions: The Palermiti clan joined the Parisi clan in 2017, forming a joint criminal society.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2017 al clan Parisi si è unito il clan Palermiti, dando vita a una 'società della guerra'

descriptions: The Palermiti clan merged with the Parisi clan to form a joint criminal society.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 2017, the Palermiti clan has joined the Parisi clan, giving life to a 'war society'

descriptions: Parisi and Mercante dissociated from Sacra Corona Unita in 1986.
edges.evidence_quote: Anche Parisi e Mercante si dissociarono dalla Sacra Corona Unita nel 1986

descriptions: Savino Parisi was a former boss of SCU before leading his own clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Savino Parisi - ex boss della SCU e capo dell' omonimo clan di Japigia

descriptions: The Parisi clan fought against the Scissionisti splinter group for control of drug markets.
edges.evidence_quote: conflitto scoppiato nel quartiere contro la compagine 'scissionista' capeggiata da Antonio Busco

descriptions: The Strisciuglio clan is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Strisciuglio clan

descriptions: Serbian criminal networks cooperated with Parisi-Palermiti clan in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 2009, an international drug trafficking operation involving the Bari -based Parisi-Palermiti clan , the 'Ndrangheta , and Serbian criminal networks has been thwarted

descriptions: Strisciuglio clan is listed as a rival of Parisi-Palermiti clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Parisi-Palermiti clan

Clan Gionta.description: Camorra clan based in Torre Annunziata, Italy, founded by Valentino Gionta in the early 1980s. Engaged in cigarette smuggling, heroin trafficking, and control of the fish market. Known for its violent rise and conflicts with other Camorra groups. Currently active despite arrests of key members.

descriptions: Gionta clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the powerful Gionta clan of the Camorra

descriptions: Alfieri clan listed Gionta clan as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Gionta clan

descriptions: Clan Alfieri carried out the Sant'Alessandro massacre against the Gionta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: un commando composto da almeno 14 persone arriva a Torre Annunziata ... uccide 8 persone appartenenti al clan Gionta

descriptions: D'Alessandro historically allied with Gionta.
edges.evidence_quote: le cui alleanze hanno attraversato la storia dei Cutolo , dei Bardellino , dei Nuvoletta e dei Gionta

descriptions: Gallo-Cavalieri and Gionta have been in a faida since December 1990, with periods of truce.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Gallo-Cavalieri e i Gionta : combattuta tra il clan Gionta e il clan Gallo-Cavalieri di Torre Annunziata

descriptions: The two clans were in a long and bloody feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Si è per lungo tempo opposto al clan Gionta , generando una sanguinosa faida che ha portato a diversi ammazzati.

descriptions: Clan Gionta entered into conflict with Antonio Bardellino's clan.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan entra in conflitto con Antonio Bardellino

descriptions: Clan Alfieri carried out a massacre against Clan Gionta.
edges.evidence_quote: la strage di Sant'Alessandro che sarà perpetrata dagli uomini di Carmine Alfieri

descriptions: Clan Gionta strengthened ties with the Chierchia clan through marriage.
edges.evidence_quote: il matrimonio del nipote omonimo del boss Valentino con una ragazza della famiglia di "François" Chierchia

descriptions: Clan Gionta was in conflict with the Gallo clan, resulting in six murders.
edges.evidence_quote: sei omicidi rivelano lo scontro dei Gionta con il clan Gallo

descriptions: Clan Gionta killed NCO leaders in Torre Annunziata.
edges.evidence_quote: fanno piazza pulita dei capizona della Nuova Camorra Organizzata di Cutolo a Torre Annunziata

descriptions: Gionta and Nuvoletta fought the Nuova Famiglia coalition in an internal faida from 1983 to 1985.
edges.evidence_quote: faida all'interno della Nuova Famiglia : combattuta tra i Nuvoletta - Gionta e la Nuova Famiglia (Alfieri, Galasso, Fabbrocino, Bardellino e Vastarella) negli anni 1983-85

descriptions: Clan Gionta sided with the Nuova Famiglia against Cutolo's NCO.
edges.evidence_quote: affiancandosi alla Nuova Famiglia nella lotta contro il disegno criminale di Raffaele Cutolo

descriptions: The Gionta clan was allied with the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: an attack at Torre Annunziata ... led to the massacre of eight members of the Gionta clan allied with the Nuvolettas

descriptions: The Gionta clan was allied with the Nuvoletta clan during the internal war.
edges.evidence_quote: the Gionta clan allied with Nuvoletta

descriptions: Clan Gionta established contacts with the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: stringe contatti con ... i Nuvoletta di Marano

descriptions: Clan Gionta established contacts with Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: stringe contatti con Cosa nostra

descriptions: Gionta clan and Società foggiana formed an alliance in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: the Italian police dismantled an international drug trafficking alliance between the powerful Gionta clan of the Camorra, the Società foggiana, also known as the fifth-mafia, and Moroccan drug traffickers.

descriptions: The Vangone-Limelli split from the Gionta clan in a bloody feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Per via di alcuni screzi e per diversità di vedute nella gestione e nel controllo delle attività illecite sul territorio i boschesi si staccarono dai Gionta. La scissione fu chiaramente sanguinosa

descriptions: The Vangone-Limelli were historically affiliated with the Gionta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il finire degli anni ’70 e l’inizio degli anni ‘80 il capostipite Luigi Limelli, i suoi affiliati e parenti della famiglia Vangone erano storici affiliati del clan Gionta di Torre Annunziata.

descriptions: Corleonesi allied with Gionta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: si allearono con i clan camorristici dei Nuvoletta , Lubrano-Ligato e Gionta

descriptions: Nuova Famiglia members killed members of the Gionta clan in a massacre.
edges.evidence_quote: uccise 8 persone appartenenti al clan Gionta

descriptions: The Gionta clan was a member of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.
edges.evidence_quote: The NF consisted of Bardellino, Michele Zaza ... the Gionta clan (from Torre Annunziata)

descriptions: Gionta clan was part of the anti-Cutolo alliance that became Nuova Famiglia.
edges.evidence_quote: Altre famiglie coinvolte nell'ampia alleanza anticutoliana furono i Bardellino, i Ferrara, gli Ammaturo, i Moccia, i Cesarano, i D'Alessandro e i Maresca di Castellammare di Stabia, i Gionta, i Gallo e i Limelli di Torre Annunziata...

descriptions: The Gionta clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.
edges.evidence_quote: The confederation included: - Valentino Gionta, boss the Gionta clan

descriptions: The Gionta clan was the closest ally of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Valentino Gionta, the closest ally of the Nuvolettas

descriptions: Nuvoletta and Gionta were allied in a coalition against Casalesi and Alfieri.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra le coalizioni Casalesi - Alfieri e Nuvoletta - Gionta

descriptions: The Gionta clan was an ally of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: città del clan Gionta , alleato dei Nuvoletta

descriptions: Nuvoletta and Gionta clans were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: La strage venne cagionata da omicidi realizzati dai Nuvoletta-Gionta.

descriptions: Cosa Nostra's Corleonesi faction had contacts with the Gionta Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Elementi di spicco dei Corleonesi (come Salvatore Riina, Leoluca Bagarella, Bernardo Provenzano, Pippo Calò, Giovanni Brusca e Gaspare Mutolo) si sono trovati a contatto con famiglie camorristiche come i Nuvoletta, Lubrano-Ligato e Gionta, invece i rivali Carmine Alfieri e Antonio Bardellino, erano alleati dei perdenti come Alfio Ferlito, Gaetano Badalamenti e Tommaso Buscetta.

descriptions: Società Foggiana was part of an international drug trafficking alliance with Gionta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the Italian police dismantled an international drug trafficking alliance between the powerful Gionta clan of the Camorra, the Società Foggiana, and Moroccan drug traffickers.

descriptions: Società Foggiana allied with Gionta clan in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: dismantled an international drug trafficking alliance between the powerful Gionta clan of the Camorra, the Società Foggiana, and Moroccan drug traffickers.

Wo Hop To.description: Wo Hop To is a triad group based in Wan Chai, Hong Kong, founded in 1908 in Sai Ying Pun as a secret political organization opposing the Qing dynasty. It specializes in protection rackets and is one of the Four Major Gangs of Hong Kong, alongside Wo Shing Wo, 14K, and Sun Yee On. The group expanded into the United States in the early 1990s, establishing a presence in San Francisco.

descriptions: Jackson Street Boys succeeded Wo Hop To after law enforcement cracked down on Wo Hop To around 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: The Jackson Boys gang was the successor to the Wo Hop To Triad which ruled the streets of San Francisco's Chinatown in the 1980s and early 1990s.

descriptions: Snakeheads are rivals with Wo Hop To.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sun Yee On , Wo Hop To

descriptions: Infobox lists Wo Hop To as an ally of Sun Yee On.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Wo Hop To, Wo On Lok, Albanian mafia, Camorra, Serbian mafia, American Mafia, Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: TRG formed alliances with Wo Hop To.
edges.evidence_quote: They have formed alliances with Chinese organizations such as the 14K, Wah Ching and Wo Hop To in California and the New York City-based Ghost Shadows.

descriptions: Wo Hop To is a triad group; Raymond Chow is a former leader of its American branch.
edges.evidence_quote: Raymond "Shrimp Boy" Chow , former leader of the American branch of Wo Hop To

descriptions: Wo Hop To hitmen killed Wah Ching leader Danny Wong.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1991, Danny 'Ah Pai' Wong, the leader of Wah Ching, was shot and killed by Wo Hop To (WHT) hitmen from Seattle Chinatown.

descriptions: Wo Hop To is listed as a rival of Wah Ching in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wo Hop To

descriptions: The Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged under an alliance led by Peter Chong and Raymond Chow.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the alliance, the Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged, and an umbrella organization, Tien Hu Wui, was formed to oversee their combined business.

descriptions: The text mentions both organizations in the context of Chow's ties, but does not assert a direct relationship between them.
edges.evidence_quote: ties to a San Francisco Chinatown street gang and an organized crime syndicate, including the American branch of the Hong Kong -based triad Wo Hop To and the Hop Sing Boys.

descriptions: Jackson Street Boys were founded by former Wah Ching members who had defected to Wo Hop To.
edges.evidence_quote: After law enforcement cracked down on the Wo Hop To around 1992, former Wah Ching members who had defected to the Wo Hop To founded the Jackson Street Boys

descriptions: Wo Hop To is affiliated with Sun Yee On.
edges.evidence_quote: Wo Hop To specialises in protection rackets and is affiliated with the Sun Yee On triad.

descriptions: Infobox lists Tiny Rascal Gang as an ally of Wo Hop To.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sun Yee On Tiny Rascal Gang

descriptions: The Wah Ching and Wo Hop To were in a violent power struggle in San Francisco.
edges.evidence_quote: At about the same time, the Wah Ching and Wo Hop To were struggling for power in San Francisco.

descriptions: Wo Hop To chased Wah Ching out of the Bay Area, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Wo Hop To chased the Wah Ching gang out of the Bay Area and the Wah Ching headed to Southern California.

descriptions: Wo Hop To killed the Wah Ching leader, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wo Hop To controlled Chinatown illegal activities after killing the Wah Ching leader Danny Wong.

descriptions: Wo Shing Wo was established as an international branch of Wo Hop To.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wo Shing Wo was established in Toronto in 1930 as an international branch of the Wo Hop To

descriptions: Stasi provided military advisers to ZANLA.
edges.evidence_quote: Other Stasi agents worked as military advisers to African Marxist guerrillas ... They included ... Robert Mugabe's ZANLA

European Kindred.description: A prison and street gang founded in 1998 at Snake River Correctional Institution in Oregon by David Patrick Kennedy and another inmate. Membership is restricted to white males with no more than one-sixteenth Native American ancestry. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, assaults, dog fighting, and murder, with an estimated 500 members in the Oregon prison system as of 2006. It operates a 'Blood in, Blood out' policy and is known for its secretive, formal structure.

descriptions: S.C.F is a rival of AFCA.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: S.C.F

descriptions: Black Jackets and Bandogs are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rock Machine , Veles gang , Black Dogs , Bandogs , 210 International , Dogpack 416 , Black Army , Holmblaaa , Kommando 210

descriptions: Puerto Rican Stones killed a Simon City Royals leader.
edges.evidence_quote: The leader of the Central Park and Wilson Simon City Royals, Todd O'Thomas, his alias was Todd Brewer, was killed by the Puerto Rican Stones in the early 80s.

Moroccan mafia.description: A collection of criminal organizations primarily composed of people of Moroccan descent, specializing in trafficking large quantities of cocaine and synthetic drugs through Spain, Portugal, the Netherlands, and Belgium. Active since the 1990s, these networks have privileged relationships with Colombian and Mexican cartels and import drugs via European ports. The group gained notoriety during the Mocro-War gang conflict starting in 2012.

descriptions: Infobox lists Mocro Maffia as a rival of Al-Zein.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mocro Maffia

descriptions: Fish Farmers clan competed against other Moroccan mafia organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: They very quickly competed against other organisations within the Moroccan mafia.

descriptions: Moroccan mafia and 'Ndrangheta are described as co-dominating the European drug market.
edges.evidence_quote: The Moroccan mafia in the Netherlands and Belgium dominate the European market for cocaine, cannabis and synthetic drugs, alongside the Irish Mob, and the Italian (’Ndrangheta and Camorra), Serbian, and Albanian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2012 a large shipment of cocaine was stolen by the Turtle clan in the Port of Antwerp. This eventually led to a gang war amongst multiple criminal organisations which the Dutch and Belgian media outlets described as the Mocro-War.

edges.evidence_quote: Alongside the Mixers clan and Turtle clan, the El Ballouti clan is held responsible for a significant number of bombs and grenades that were thrown in several neighbourhoods of Antwerp
